---
title: "Lectures"
---

Each week has a slide deck and one or two short in-class R exercises. In a deck, press **`f`**
for fullscreen, **`o`** for a slide overview, and **`s`** for the speaker view. Every lecture
below lists its **required and optional readings** — start there.

::: callout-start
This is the new site in progress. **Lectures 1, 2, 3, and 6** are fully migrated — the rest are
being ported to the same format, but their readings are already listed so you can prepare ahead.
:::

```{=html}
<div class="lectures">

  <div class="block-label">Getting ready</div>

  <div class="lecture">
    <div class="num">0</div>
    <div class="body"><span class="t">Setup — R, RStudio &amp; your project</span>
      <div class="links"><a href="about.html">Setup instructions</a></div>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="block-label">Foundations — regression, and why causation is hard</div>

  <div class="lecture">
    <div class="num">1</div>
    <div class="body"><span class="t">Introduction</span>
      <div class="links"><a href="1-Introduction.html">Slides</a></div>
      <ul class="readings">
        <li><span class="rtag rtag-read">Read</span>Angrist, Joshua D., and Jörn-Steffen Pischke. 2014. <em>Mastering ’Metrics: The Path from Cause to Effect</em>. Princeton, NJ: Princeton University Press. — <strong>Introduction (pp. xi–xv)</strong>.</li>
        <li><span class="rtag rtag-read">Read</span>Johnson, Tim, Dalton Conley, and Christopher T. Dawes. 2019. “<a href="https://www.theatlantic.com/science/archive/2019/12/vietnam-draft-lotteries-were-scientific-experiment/602842/">The Accidental Experiment That Changed Men’s Lives</a>.” <em>The Atlantic</em>, December 2.</li>
      </ul>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="lecture">
    <div class="num">2</div>
    <div class="body"><span class="t">Correlation &amp; regression</span>
      <div class="links">
        <a href="2-Corr-n-Reg.html">Slides</a>
        <a href="2-exercise1.html">Exercise 1</a>
        <a href="2-exercise2.html">Exercise 2</a>
      </div>
      <ul class="readings">
        <li><span class="rtag rtag-read">Read</span>De Veaux, Richard D., Paul F. Velleman, and David E. Bock. 2021. <em>Stats: Data and Models</em>. Global ed. Harlow, UK: Pearson. — <strong>Ch. 6.1–6.2 &amp; 7.1–7.3</strong>.</li>
      </ul>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="lecture">
    <div class="num">3</div>
    <div class="body"><span class="t">Randomness &amp; statistical inference</span>
      <div class="links">
        <a href="3-Random.html">Slides</a>
        <a href="3-exercise1.html">Exercise 1</a>
        <a href="3-exercise2.html">Exercise 2</a>
      </div>
      <ul class="readings">
        <li><span class="rtag rtag-read">Read</span>De Veaux, Richard D., Paul F. Velleman, and David E. Bock. 2021. <em>Stats: Data and Models</em>. Global ed. Harlow, UK: Pearson. — <strong>Ch. 17.1–17.3, 18.1–18.3 &amp; 19.2</strong>.</li>
      </ul>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="lecture soon">
    <div class="num">4</div>
    <div class="body"><span class="t">OLS wisdoms</span>
      <span class="badge-soon">coming soon</span>
      <ul class="readings">
        <li><span class="rtag rtag-read">Read</span>De Veaux, Richard D., Paul F. Velleman, and David E. Bock. 2021. <em>Stats: Data and Models</em>. Global ed. Harlow, UK: Pearson. — <strong>Ch. 9.5</strong>.</li>
      </ul>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="lecture soon">
    <div class="num">5</div>
    <div class="body"><span class="t">Selection bias</span>
      <span class="badge-soon">coming soon</span>
      <ul class="readings">
        <li><span class="rtag rtag-read">Read</span>Angrist, Joshua D., and Jörn-Steffen Pischke. 2014. <em>Mastering ’Metrics: The Path from Cause to Effect</em>. Princeton, NJ: Princeton University Press. — <strong>Ch. 1 (pp. 1–11)</strong>.</li>
        <li><span class="rtag rtag-skim">Skim</span>Schaeffer, Merlin, and Judith Kas. 2024. “The Integration Paradox: Does Awareness of the Extent of Ethno-Racial Discrimination Increase Reports of Discrimination?” <em>Political Psychology</em> 46(3):623–636. doi:<a href="https://doi.org/10.1111/pops.13027">10.1111/pops.13027</a>.</li>
      </ul>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="block-label">The experimental ideal</div>

  <div class="lecture">
    <div class="num">6</div>
    <div class="body"><span class="t">Randomized controlled trials (RCTs)</span>
      <div class="links">
        <a href="6-RCTees.html">Slides</a>
        <a href="6-exercise1.html">Exercise 1</a>
        <a href="6-exercise2.html">Exercise 2</a>
      </div>
      <ul class="readings">
        <li><span class="rtag rtag-read">Read</span>Angrist, Joshua D., and Jörn-Steffen Pischke. 2014. <em>Mastering ’Metrics: The Path from Cause to Effect</em>. Princeton, NJ: Princeton University Press. — <strong>Ch. 1 (pp. 12–32)</strong>.</li>
        <li><span class="rtag rtag-skim">Skim</span>Schaeffer, Merlin, and Judith Kas. 2024. “The Integration Paradox: Does Awareness of the Extent of Ethno-Racial Discrimination Increase Reports of Discrimination?” <em>Political Psychology</em> 46(3):623–636. doi:<a href="https://doi.org/10.1111/pops.13027">10.1111/pops.13027</a>.</li>
      </ul>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="lecture soon">
    <div class="num">7</div>
    <div class="body"><span class="t">Natural experiments &amp; instrumental variables</span>
      <span class="badge-soon">coming soon</span>
      <ul class="readings">
        <li><span class="rtag rtag-read">Read</span>Angrist, Joshua D., and Jörn-Steffen Pischke. 2014. <em>Mastering ’Metrics: The Path from Cause to Effect</em>. Princeton, NJ: Princeton University Press. — <strong>Ch. 3 (pp. 98–123)</strong>.</li>
        <li><span class="rtag rtag-skim">Skim</span>Legewie, Joscha. 2013. “Terrorist Events and Attitudes toward Immigrants: A Natural Experiment.” <em>American Journal of Sociology</em> 118(5):1199–1245. doi:<a href="https://doi.org/10.1086/669605">10.1086/669605</a>.</li>
        <li><span class="rtag rtag-skim">Skim</span>Sherman, Lawrence W., and Richard A. Berk. 1984. “The Specific Deterrent Effects of Arrest for Domestic Assault.” <em>American Sociological Review</em> 49(2):261–272. doi:<a href="https://doi.org/10.2307/2095575">10.2307/2095575</a>.</li>
        <li><span class="rtag rtag-skim">Skim</span>Chetty, Raj, Nathaniel Hendren, and Lawrence F. Katz. 2016. “The Effects of Exposure to Better Neighborhoods on Children: New Evidence from the Moving to Opportunity Experiment.” <em>American Economic Review</em> 106(4):855–902. doi:<a href="https://doi.org/10.1257/aer.20150572">10.1257/aer.20150572</a>.</li>
      </ul>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="block-label">Multiple regression &amp; quasi-experimental designs</div>

  <div class="lecture soon">
    <div class="num">8</div>
    <div class="body"><span class="t">Multiple OLS regression</span>
      <span class="badge-soon">coming soon</span>
      <ul class="readings">
        <li><span class="rtag rtag-read">Read</span>De Veaux, Richard D., Paul F. Velleman, and David E. Bock. 2021. <em>Stats: Data and Models</em>. Global ed. Harlow, UK: Pearson. — <strong>Ch. 9.1–9.4</strong>.</li>
      </ul>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="lecture soon">
    <div class="num">9</div>
    <div class="body"><span class="t">Multiple OLS in practice</span>
      <span class="badge-soon">coming soon</span>
      <ul class="readings">
        <li><span class="rtag rtag-read">Read</span>Angrist, Joshua D., and Jörn-Steffen Pischke. 2014. <em>Mastering ’Metrics: The Path from Cause to Effect</em>. Princeton, NJ: Princeton University Press. — <strong>Ch. 2 (Regression)</strong>.</li>
      </ul>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="lecture soon">
    <div class="num">10</div>
    <div class="body"><span class="t">Instrumental variables &amp; 2SLS</span>
      <span class="badge-soon">coming soon</span>
      <ul class="readings">
        <li><span class="rtag rtag-read">Read</span>Angrist, Joshua D., and Jörn-Steffen Pischke. 2014. <em>Mastering ’Metrics: The Path from Cause to Effect</em>. Princeton, NJ: Princeton University Press. — <strong>Ch. 3 (pp. 123–146)</strong>.</li>
      </ul>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="lecture soon">
    <div class="num">11</div>
    <div class="body"><span class="t">Interactions &amp; conditional effects</span>
      <span class="badge-soon">coming soon</span>
      <ul class="readings">
        <li><span class="rtag rtag-read">Read</span>De Veaux, Richard D., Paul F. Velleman, and David E. Bock. 2021. <em>Stats: Data and Models</em>. Global ed. Harlow, UK: Pearson. — <strong>pp. 324–329</strong>.</li>
      </ul>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="lecture soon">
    <div class="num">12</div>
    <div class="body"><span class="t">Polynomials</span>
      <span class="badge-soon">coming soon</span>
      <ul class="readings">
        <li><span class="rtag rtag-read">Read</span>Huntington-Klein, Nick. 2022. <em>The Effect: An Introduction to Research Design and Causality</em>. Boca Raton, FL: CRC Press. — <strong>Ch. 13.2.2–13.2.3</strong> (free online: <a href="https://theeffectbook.net/ch-StatisticalAdjustment.html#polynomials">13.2.2 Polynomials</a>, <a href="https://theeffectbook.net/ch-StatisticalAdjustment.html#variable-transformations">13.2.3 Variable Transformations</a>).</li>
      </ul>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="lecture soon">
    <div class="num">13</div>
    <div class="body"><span class="t">Regression discontinuity design (RDD)</span>
      <span class="badge-soon">coming soon</span>
      <ul class="readings">
        <li><span class="rtag rtag-read">Read</span>Angrist, Joshua D., and Jörn-Steffen Pischke. 2014. <em>Mastering ’Metrics: The Path from Cause to Effect</em>. Princeton, NJ: Princeton University Press. — <strong>Ch. 4 (Regression Discontinuity Designs)</strong>.</li>
      </ul>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="block-label">Wrap-up</div>

  <div class="lecture soon">
    <div class="num">14</div>
    <div class="body"><span class="t">Conclusion</span>
      <span class="badge-soon">coming soon</span>
      <span class="reading-none">— no assigned reading</span></div>
  </div>

</div>
```
